journeys.name: Login with Email and Password
journeys.description: Test the complete authentication flow including login form, JWT storage, and redirect to dashboard

steps.expected_result: Unauthenticated user is redirected to /login
steps.visual_check: Login page visible with Nexa logo, email and password fields, 'Sign In' button, and purple theme branding. Card centered on desktop.

steps.expected_result: Form fields populated with email and password
Login API called, JWT received, user redirected to dashboard
steps.visual_check: Dashboard or authenticated home page loaded. App shell visible with sidebar on left, header bar at top, and main content area.

steps.expected_result: User avatar/initials visible in the header, confirming authenticated state

journeys.name: Login with MFA Challenge
journeys.description: Test the MFA verification step when the server requires a TOTP code

steps.expected_result: Login page loads
Form fields populated
Server returns mfaRequired: true, MFA code input appears
steps.visual_check: MFA verification step visible with 6-digit TOTP code input field and 'Verify' button.

steps.expected_result: 6-digit code entered in the TOTP field
MFA verified, user redirected to authenticated dashboard
steps.visual_check: Authenticated app shell loaded with sidebar and header. MFA challenge no longer visible.

journeys.name: Unauthenticated Access Redirects to Login
journeys.description: Verify that accessing any authenticated route without a session redirects to /login

steps.expected_result: User is redirected to /login since they have no valid JWT
steps.visual_check: Login page displayed. URL shows /login. No sidebar or app shell visible.

steps.expected_result: User is redirected to /login
User is redirected to /login

journeys.name: Sidebar Module Navigation
journeys.description: Test sidebar module groups, expansion/collapse, sub-item navigation, and active route highlighting

steps.expected_result: Authenticated dashboard loads with sidebar visible
steps.visual_check: Full app shell with sidebar on the left showing module groups (System, Finance, Sales, etc.) with Lucide icons and translated labels.

steps.expected_result: Finance module group expands to show sub-items
steps.visual_check: Finance group expanded showing sub-items: Chart of Accounts, Journals, Financial Periods, Bank Reconciliation, Budgets. Each with a Lucide icon and label.

steps.expected_result: Navigation to /finance/journals, Journals item highlighted in sidebar
steps.visual_check: Journals item has active styling: bold text, purple left border, light purple background. Main content area shows Journals page (placeholder).

steps.expected_result: Sales group expands, Finance group may remain expanded or collapse
Navigation to /sales/quotes, previous Journals highlight removed, Quotes now highlighted
steps.visual_check: Sales > Quotes item is now active with purple highlight. Finance > Journals is no longer highlighted.

steps.target: Sidebar collapse toggle button (chevron at bottom)
steps.expected_result: Sidebar collapses to 64px icon-only mode
steps.visual_check: Sidebar collapsed to narrow width showing only icons. Main content area expanded to fill space. Collapse chevron now points right.

steps.expected_result: Sidebar expands back to 256px full mode

journeys.name: Switch Company Context
journeys.description: Test company switching from the sidebar, verifying global context update and permissions re-fetch

steps.expected_result: Dashboard loads with current company shown in sidebar header
steps.visual_check: Company switcher at top of sidebar showing current company name and logo/initials.

steps.expected_result: Dropdown opens listing available companies with current company checked
steps.visual_check: Dropdown menu showing list of companies. Current company has a checkmark indicator. Other companies listed without checkmark.

steps.target: Second company in the dropdown list
steps.expected_result: Company context switches, toast confirms switch, permissions re-fetched
steps.visual_check: Success toast visible showing 'Switched to [Company Name]'. Sidebar header now shows the new company name. Sidebar modules may update based on new permissions.

journeys.name: User Menu and Sign Out
journeys.description: Test the user avatar dropdown menu items and the sign out flow

steps.expected_result: Authenticated dashboard loads

steps.target: User avatar button in the top-right header
steps.expected_result: User dropdown menu opens
steps.visual_check: Dropdown showing: user's full name at top, role and company name below, divider, 'My Profile' link, 'Preferences' link, divider, 'Sign Out' button.

steps.expected_result: User signed out, auth cleared, redirected to /login
steps.visual_check: Login page displayed. No sidebar or app shell visible. Session fully cleared.

journeys.name: Responsive Sidebar Behaviour
journeys.description: Test sidebar collapse on tablet width and off-canvas drawer on mobile width

steps.expected_result: Full sidebar at desktop width (>=1280px)
steps.visual_check: Full sidebar (256px) with icon + label visible for all module groups.

steps.expected_result: Sidebar auto-collapses to icon-only mode (64px)
steps.visual_check: Sidebar collapsed to icons only. Main content area fills remaining space. Tooltips should appear on icon hover.

steps.expected_result: Sidebar hidden. Hamburger menu visible in header.
steps.visual_check: No sidebar visible. Header shows hamburger menu icon on the left. Content fills full width.

steps.expected_result: Off-canvas sidebar drawer slides in from left
steps.visual_check: Sidebar overlay/sheet visible from left with full navigation. Backdrop overlay behind. Module groups and company switcher visible.

steps.target: System module group in mobile drawer
steps.expected_result: System group expands showing sub-items
Navigation to /system/users, drawer closes automatically
steps.visual_check: Mobile drawer closed. Main content shows Users page. Hamburger still visible in header.

journeys.name: Co-Pilot Dock: Cmd+K and Drawer
journeys.description: Test the unified search input focus via keyboard shortcut and the Co-Pilot drawer open/close

steps.expected_result: Authenticated dashboard loads with search input in header
Unified search input in header gains focus, command palette dropdown may open
steps.visual_check: Search input in header bar is focused with cursor blinking. A command palette dropdown or autocomplete area may be visible below the input.

steps.expected_result: Autocomplete dropdown shows matching entity suggestions for invoices
steps.visual_check: Autocomplete dropdown showing entity results section with invoice matches. May also show page results and AI prompt suggestions sections.

steps.target: Press Escape key to close search
steps.expected_result: Search dropdown closes, input blurs
Co-Pilot drawer opens from the right side
steps.visual_check: 380px wide drawer visible on right side. Main content area resized to accommodate. Drawer shows chat history selector, conversation area (empty or with welcome), quick prompts, and input area at bottom.

steps.expected_result: Message typed in the Co-Pilot input field
User message appears right-aligned with purple background. AI response begins streaming with typing indicator.
steps.visual_check: Chat area showing user message bubble on right (purple background) and AI response streaming on left (grey background) with pulsing dot typing indicator.

steps.target: Chat button in header to close drawer
steps.expected_result: Co-Pilot drawer closes, main content area resizes back to full width
steps.visual_check: Drawer closed. Main content fills the full width again. Chat button in header no longer showing active state.

journeys.name: Browse Resource Registry with Filters
journeys.description: Test the read-only Resource Registry page with module and type filters, search, and pagination

steps.expected_result: Resource Registry page loads with T1 Entity List layout
steps.visual_check: Page title 'Resource Registry'. Breadcrumbs: System > Resource Registry. Data table with columns: Code, Name, Module, Type, Sort Order. No [+ New] button visible. Module and Type filter dropdowns visible.

steps.expected_result: [+ New] button does NOT exist (read-only page)
Dropdown opens showing 'All Modules' plus all known modules (system, finance, sales, etc.)
steps.visual_check: Module filter dropdown open showing options: All Modules, System, Finance, Accounts Receivable, Accounts Payable, Sales, Purchasing, Inventory, CRM, HR & Payroll, Manufacturing, Reporting.

steps.expected_result: Table filters to show only finance module resources
steps.visual_check: Data table now shows only rows where Module column is 'Finance'. Filter dropdown shows 'Finance' selected.

steps.expected_result: Dropdown opens showing 'All Types' plus PAGE, REPORT, SETTING, MAINTENANCE
Table filters further to show only finance PAGE resources (AND logic)
steps.visual_check: Table shows only resources where Module=Finance AND Type=PAGE. Both filter dropdowns show their active selections.

steps.expected_result: Table filters by search term across code, name, description fields (300ms debounce)
steps.visual_check: Table shows resources matching 'journal' in the finance/PAGE context. Search input shows 'journal' text.

steps.expected_result: Nothing happens (read-only page, no row click navigation)

journeys.name: Create a New Access Group
journeys.description: Test the full access group creation flow: navigate to create page, fill form, handle validation, save

steps.expected_result: Access Group list page loads with T1 Entity List
steps.visual_check: Page title 'Access Groups'. Breadcrumbs: System > Access Groups. Data table with columns: Code, Name, System badge, Users, Created. [+ New] button visible in action area.

steps.expected_result: Navigates to /system/access-groups/new create form
steps.visual_check: Create Access Group form with fields: Code (text input with placeholder 'e.g., SALES_MGR'), Name (text input), Description (textarea, optional). Breadcrumbs: System > Access Groups > New. Create and Cancel buttons.

steps.expected_result: Code auto-transforms to uppercase: TEST_SALES_REP. Name and description filled.
POST /system/access-groups called, on success redirect to detail page with success toast
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Access group created successfully'. Redirected to access group detail page showing 'TEST_SALES_REP' as code, 'Test Sales Representative' as name. Permissions and Field Overrides tabs visible.

journeys.name: Access Group Create - Duplicate Code Error
journeys.description: Test 409 CONFLICT handling when creating an access group with a code that already exists

steps.expected_result: Create Access Group form loads
Form filled with existing system group code
API returns 409 CONFLICT, inline error appears on Code field
steps.visual_check: Code field shows inline error message: 'An access group with this code already exists'. Form remains on the create page for correction.

journeys.name: Configure Access Group Permission Matrix
journeys.description: Test the permission matrix checkbox grid on the access group detail page: check/uncheck permissions, select-all per module, save

steps.expected_result: Access Group list page loads

steps.target: Row for the test access group created in j10
steps.expected_result: Navigates to access group detail page
Permission matrix loads with checkbox grid grouped by module
steps.visual_check: Permission matrix showing collapsible module groups. Each expanded group shows resource names with columns: Access, New, View, Edit, Delete. Checkbox grid visible with unchecked boxes.

steps.target: Expand 'Sales' module group in permission matrix
steps.expected_result: Sales module section expands showing sales resources
steps.visual_check: Sales module section expanded showing resources like Quotes, Orders, Delivery Notes with checkbox columns for each permission flag.

steps.target: 'Select All canAccess' checkbox on Sales module header
steps.expected_result: All sales resources get canAccess=true
steps.visual_check: All checkboxes in the 'Access' column for sales resources are now checked.

steps.expected_result: canView toggled to true for Sales Orders
canEdit toggled to true for Sales Orders
PUT /system/access-groups/:id/permissions called with full permissions array. Success toast shown.
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Permissions saved successfully'. Save button returns to disabled state. Checkboxes retain their checked state.

journeys.name: Deactivate a Custom Access Group
journeys.description: Test deactivation of a non-system access group via overflow menu with confirmation dialog

steps.expected_result: Access Group list loads

steps.target: Row for the test access group (TEST_SALES_REP)
steps.expected_result: Navigates to detail page
Overflow menu opens showing 'Deactivate' option
steps.visual_check: Dropdown menu with 'Deactivate' option visible. Since this is a custom group (not system), the option is enabled.

steps.expected_result: Confirmation dialog appears with warning about consequences
steps.visual_check: Confirmation dialog with title 'Deactivate Access Group', body text 'Deactivate TEST_SALES_REP? Users assigned to this group will lose its permissions.' Cancel and Deactivate buttons visible.

steps.expected_result: DELETE /system/access-groups/:id called (soft-delete), redirect to list with success toast
steps.visual_check: Redirected to access group list. Success toast 'Access group deactivated'. The deactivated group is no longer in the default list view.

journeys.name: System Access Group Protection Rules
journeys.description: Verify that system access groups show a protection banner and have Deactivate disabled

steps.expected_result: Access Group list loads showing system groups with 'System' badge
steps.visual_check: List showing access groups. System groups have a 'System' badge in the System column.

steps.target: Row for a system access group (e.g., FULL_ACCESS)
steps.expected_result: Detail page loads with system group banner
steps.visual_check: Info banner at top of page: 'This is a system access group. Its code cannot be changed and it cannot be deactivated.' Code field is read-only. System badge visible next to group name.

steps.expected_result: Overflow menu opens but Deactivate is disabled/hidden
steps.visual_check: Overflow menu open. Deactivate option is either disabled (greyed out) or not present for system groups.

journeys.name: User List and Access Group Assignment
journeys.description: Test viewing users, navigating to user detail, and assigning/removing access groups

steps.expected_result: User list page loads with T1 Entity List layout
steps.visual_check: Page title 'Users'. Data table with columns: Name, Email, Role (with coloured badges), Access Groups count, Status (Active/Inactive badges), Last Login. No [+ New] button (users created via auth registration).

steps.expected_result: User list filters by name or email matching 'admin'
steps.visual_check: Table filtered to show users matching 'admin'. Search input shows the text.

steps.target: First user row in the filtered list
steps.expected_result: Navigates to /system/users/:id user detail page
steps.visual_check: User detail page showing: user name as title, email, role badge, status badge, last login. Below: Access Groups assignment panel showing currently assigned groups as removable tag/chips.

steps.expected_result: Combobox opens showing available access groups (excluding already assigned ones)
steps.visual_check: Popover with searchable combobox listing active access groups. Already-assigned groups are excluded from the list. Search input for filtering.

steps.expected_result: Combobox filters to groups matching 'VIEW'
Group added as a tag in the assignment panel (optimistic, unsaved)
steps.visual_check: New access group tag 'VIEW_ONLY' appears in the assignment panel alongside existing group tags. Save button is now enabled.

steps.expected_result: PUT /system/users/:id/access-groups called with full accessGroupIds array. Success toast shown.
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Access groups updated successfully'. Save button returns to disabled state. Tags still show all assigned groups.

steps.target: Remove (X) button on the VIEW_ONLY tag
steps.expected_result: Tag removed from local state, Save button enabled
steps.visual_check: VIEW_ONLY tag removed from assignment panel. Save button is now enabled again indicating unsaved changes.

steps.expected_result: Groups saved without VIEW_ONLY, success toast

journeys.name: Configure Field Overrides on Access Group
journeys.description: Test the Field Overrides tab on access group detail: select resource, add override, set visibility, save

steps.expected_result: Access group list loads

steps.target: Row for a custom access group
steps.expected_result: Navigates to access group detail page
Field Overrides panel loads with resource selector and empty state
steps.visual_check: Field Overrides tab active. Resource selector combobox visible. Empty state message: 'Select a resource to configure field visibility overrides'. No field override table shown.

steps.expected_result: Popover opens with searchable list of resources grouped by module
steps.visual_check: Combobox dropdown showing resources grouped by module headers. Each resource shows name and code. Search input at top for filtering.

steps.target: Select a resource (e.g., 'Sales Orders' in Sales group)
steps.expected_result: Resource selected, empty override table shown for this resource
steps.visual_check: Selected resource name displayed. Table area shows 'No field overrides configured for this resource' with 'Add Field Override' button.

steps.expected_result: New row added with empty field path input and VISIBLE default visibility
steps.visual_check: New row in the table with: empty field path input (auto-focused), Visibility dropdown showing 'Visible', and remove (X) button.

steps.target: Field path input on new row
steps.expected_result: Field path set to 'costPrice'

steps.target: Visibility dropdown on the costPrice row
steps.expected_result: Dropdown opens showing: Visible, Read Only, Hidden
Visibility changed to HIDDEN for costPrice field
steps.visual_check: Row shows: Field Path='costPrice', Visibility='Hidden'. Save Field Overrides button is now enabled.

steps.expected_result: PUT /system/access-groups/:id/field-overrides called. Success toast shown.
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Field overrides saved successfully'. Save button returns to disabled state. Override row persists showing costPrice=HIDDEN.

journeys.name: Permission-Driven Sidebar Filtering
journeys.description: Verify that sidebar only shows modules the user has canAccess permission for

steps.expected_result: Login page loads
Login as user with limited module access (e.g., only system and sales enabled)
Authenticated, dashboard loads with filtered sidebar
steps.visual_check: Sidebar shows only modules the user has access to (e.g., System, Sales). Modules like Finance, Manufacturing, HR are NOT visible in the sidebar.

steps.expected_result: Finance module group is NOT present in the sidebar (user lacks canAccess)
Sales module group IS present in the sidebar (user has canAccess)

journeys.name: Route Guard: 403 Access Denied Page
journeys.description: Verify that navigating to a route the user lacks permissions for shows the 403 Access Denied page

steps.expected_result: User without finance module access is redirected to /403 Access Denied page
steps.visual_check: 403 Access Denied page displayed with translated error message. Link back to home page visible. App shell (sidebar/header) still visible around the error content.

steps.expected_result: Navigates back to the home/dashboard page

journeys.name: Non-Admin User Blocked from RBAC Admin Pages
journeys.description: Verify that non-ADMIN users get 403 when accessing /system/resources, /system/access-groups, etc.

steps.expected_result: Login page loads
Login as STAFF role user (non-ADMIN)
Authenticated as STAFF user
403 Access Denied page shown (ADMIN required)
steps.visual_check: 403 Access Denied page. User cannot see the Resource Registry because they lack ADMIN role.

steps.expected_result: 403 Access Denied page shown (ADMIN required)
403 Access Denied page shown (ADMIN required)

journeys.name: Export Company Configuration as JSON
journeys.description: Test the export flow: navigate to Company Profile, open export dialog, preview, download

steps.expected_result: Company Profile page loads with overflow menu
steps.visual_check: Company Profile page with company name as title. Breadcrumbs: System > Company Profile. Overflow menu button visible in the action area.

steps.expected_result: Overflow menu opens showing Export Config and Import Config
steps.visual_check: Dropdown menu showing 'Export Config' and 'Import Config' options in the Data section.

steps.expected_result: Export preview dialog opens
steps.visual_check: Dialog titled 'Export Company Configuration' with description text. Summary preview showing counts: access groups, resources, VAT codes, payment terms, number series, currencies. 'Download JSON' and 'Cancel' buttons.

steps.expected_result: API called, JSON file downloaded, success toast, dialog closes
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Configuration exported successfully'. Dialog closed. Browser download initiated for company-defaults-{slug}.json file.

journeys.name: Import Configuration with Dry Run Preview
journeys.description: Test the import flow with dry run: open dialog, upload file, run dry run, view results

steps.expected_result: Company Profile page loads
Overflow menu opens
Import dialog opens
steps.visual_check: Dialog titled 'Import Company Configuration'. File drop zone with dashed border visible. JSON textarea below with 'OR' separator. 'Dry Run' checkbox checked by default. 'Import' button disabled (no data yet). 'Cancel' button visible.

steps.expected_result: Valid JSON entered, Import button becomes enabled
steps.visual_check: JSON textarea filled with configuration JSON. Import button is now enabled (no longer disabled). Dry Run checkbox still checked.

steps.expected_result: POST /system/company-profile/import-defaults called with dryRun: true. Results panel displayed.
steps.visual_check: Results panel showing 'Dry Run Preview' title. Summary table with columns: Entity Type, Created, Updated. Rows for Resources, Access Groups, Permissions, etc. 'Apply' button to proceed with actual import. 'Close' button to dismiss.

steps.target: Apply button in dry run results
steps.expected_result: POST called with dryRun: false. Applied results shown. Success toast.
steps.visual_check: Results panel updated to 'Import Results' title. Summary shows final applied counts. Success toast 'Configuration imported successfully'. 'Done' button visible to close dialog.

steps.expected_result: Import dialog closes

journeys.name: SUPER_ADMIN Sees All Modules and Routes
journeys.description: Verify that SUPER_ADMIN bypasses all permission checks: sees all sidebar items, can access all routes

steps.expected_result: Login page loads
Login as SUPER_ADMIN user
Authenticated as SUPER_ADMIN
steps.visual_check: Sidebar shows ALL module groups: System, Finance, AR, AP, Sales, Purchasing, Inventory, CRM, HR & Payroll, Manufacturing, Reporting. None are hidden.

steps.expected_result: Route loads normally, no 403 redirect
Resource Registry page loads for SUPER_ADMIN
Access Groups page loads for SUPER_ADMIN
steps.visual_check: Access Groups page loaded successfully. All RBAC admin pages accessible to SUPER_ADMIN regardless of access group assignments.

journeys.name: Breadcrumb Navigation and Skip Link
journeys.description: Test breadcrumb trail rendering and skip-to-content accessibility link

steps.expected_result: Resource Registry page loads with breadcrumbs
steps.visual_check: Breadcrumb trail showing: System > Resource Registry. The last item (current page) has aria-current='page'. Separator characters visible between segments.

steps.expected_result: Navigates to /system (System module root)
Page loads
Breadcrumb shows System > Access Groups

steps.target: Tab key to focus 'Skip to content' link (visually hidden until focused)
steps.expected_result: Skip to content link becomes visible on focus
steps.visual_check: 'Skip to content' link visible at top of page after receiving keyboard focus. Purple focus ring visible around the link.

journeys.name: ActionBar Status-Driven Actions Demo
journeys.description: Test the ActionBar component showing different primary actions based on entity status, overflow menu sections, and confirmation dialog for destructive actions. This uses a page that renders ActionBar (once business module pages exist).

steps.expected_result: Access group list with action bar elements visible

steps.target: Row for a custom (non-system) access group
steps.expected_result: Detail page loads with Save button and overflow menu
steps.visual_check: Detail page with action area showing Save button (primary), and overflow menu trigger (three dots). Save button disabled when no changes.

steps.expected_result: Overflow menu opens with grouped action sections
steps.visual_check: Overflow dropdown showing Deactivate option. Sections with dividers visible. Keyboard shortcut hints may appear next to items.

steps.expected_result: Confirmation dialog opens for this destructive action
steps.visual_check: Destructive confirmation dialog with title, entity name in description, Cancel (ghost) and Deactivate (red/destructive) buttons.

steps.expected_result: Dialog closes, no action taken, still on detail page

journeys.name: Login Form Validation and Error Handling
journeys.description: Test form validation on the login page: empty fields, invalid credentials, error display

steps.expected_result: Login page loads
Validation errors shown for required email and password fields
steps.visual_check: Email field shows required validation error. Password field shows required validation error. Error messages are translated via i18n.

steps.expected_result: Form fields populated with invalid credentials
API returns authentication error, error toast or inline error shown
steps.visual_check: Error toast or inline error message displaying translated authentication error. Login form still visible for retry.
